evals.prompt: I want to build a URL shortener service. It should let users paste long URLs and get a short one back, track click counts, and let them see a dashboard of their links. I'm a solo developer and want to ship this in about 6 weeks.
evals.expected_output: Claude runs all 4 phases: asks discovery questions across multiple turns, proposes a tech stack with rationale, generates PROJECT.md (with ≥3 out-of-scope items), JOURNAL.md, .gitignore, and tmp/README.md.
expectations: PROJECT.md was created with a Purpose, Users, In Scope, Out of Scope, Tech Stack, and Last Updated section
Out of Scope section in PROJECT.md has at least 3 concrete items
JOURNAL.md was created with an initial dated entry
JOURNAL.md entry includes a 'Key decisions made today' section with tech stack rationale
Tech stack choices in PROJECT.md include a 'why' rationale tied to the 6-week constraint or solo developer context

evals.prompt: I want to build a task management app for my team. It needs to have tasks, assignees, due dates, and status tracking.
evals.expected_output: Claude pushes back during Phase 1 Round 3 to get at least 3 explicit out-of-scope items before proceeding. It should not skip ahead to tech stack or file generation without establishing clear scope boundaries.
expectations: Claude explicitly asks for out-of-scope items before proceeding to Phase 2
Claude does not proceed to Phase 2 without at least 3 out-of-scope items confirmed
PROJECT.md Out of Scope section has at least 3 items if files are eventually generated
Claude presents a Phase 1 summary and waits for explicit confirmation before moving to Phase 2

evals.prompt: Let's initialize a new project. I want to build a CLI tool in Go that helps developers search and replace text across large codebases, with regex support and git-aware filtering.
evals.expected_output: Claude runs all 4 phases. In Phase 2, it recommends Go (not TypeScript/Python) because the user specified Go. In Phase 4, it runs actual npx skills find searches and does not hardcode or invent skill names or install counts.
expectations: Tech stack in PROJECT.md uses Go as the primary language
Phase 4 runs npx skills find commands rather than presenting a hardcoded list
PROJECT.md was created with all required sections
